issue_title: IDP link username is not updated
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

We observed that when a user is linked to an IDP, and the username at the IDP is changed, the IDP link username in Keycloak will never get updated. (Even when the user re-logins and the IDP is configured with sync mode `force`).

The IDP link username seems to be displayed in the following places and it therefore would be great if it would be up-to-date:

- Admin UI: User info, tab Identity Provider Links
- in the Account UI (Account Security -> Linked accounts)

### Version

18.0.2

### Expected behavior

If a user logs in via IDP and the IDP's sync mode is `force`, the IDP link username should be updated, if the username changed at the IDP (i.e. the IDP token contains a username different from the one already stored at Keycloak).

### Actual behavior

The IDP link username is stored once (at first login) and is never updated.

### How to Reproduce?

The issue can be reproduced with two Keycloak realms. One of the realms (let's call it _consumer_) uses the other realm (let's call it _provider_) as IDP.

1. Create realm _provider_. Make sure that the option "Edit username" is enabled for the realm.
2. In realm _provider_, create a user with a name like _test_ with password credentials.
3. In realm _provider_, create a client with a name like _login-client_, with "Standard Flow Enabled" and "Access Type" _confidential_. For the purpose of this test, just allow the whole keycloak instance as redirect uri, by specifying "Root URL" accordingly (e.g. http://localhost:8180/auth).
4. Create realm _consumer_.
5. In realm _consumer_, create an IDP of type _keycloak-oidc_. Specify "Sync Mode" as _force_, and specify the required fields appropriately, e.g.:
   - Authorization URL: http://localhost:8180/auth/realms/provider/protocol/openid-connect/auth
   - Token URL: http://localhost:8180/auth/realms/provider/protocol/openid-connect/token
   - Client Authentication: Client secret as post
   - Client OD: login-client
   - Client Secret: <secret of login-client>

6. Login at the _consumer_ realm, by using the IDP _keycloak-oidc_ (_provider_ realm). For example you can use the account console to login: http://localhost:8180/auth/realms/consumer/account/. Fill out the user profile form with arbitrary values.
7. Check (via Admin UI) that the user has been created in the _consumer_ realm and it contains an IDP Link entry with "Provider Username"  _test_.
8. Logout from both _consumer_ and _provider_ realm (clearing cookies also works).
9. Change the username of user _test_ in the _provider_ realm, e.g. to _test1_.
10. Again login at the _consumer_ realm, using the IDP.
11. When checking the IDP link entry of the _consumer_ realm user (via Admin UI), it still has "Provider Username"  _test_, but it SHOULD BE _test1_.


### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Update IDP link username when sync mode is "force"

Closes #13049

issue_title: Operator CRD status incompatible with kstatus
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

When using the new operator with Flux, the create Keycloak CRD instance never reaches ready status because the reported status deviates from the convention otherwise used: It uses yaml booleans instead of yaml strings.

(also see [kstatus](https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/cli-utils/tree/master/pkg/kstatus)).

The conventions seems to be yaml strings:

Certificate from cert-manager:
```yaml
status:
  conditions:
    - lastTransitionTime: '2022-07-13T06:51:51Z'
      message: Certificate is up to date and has not expired
      observedGeneration: 1
      reason: Ready
      status: 'True'
      type: Ready
  notAfter: '2022-10-11T06:51:51Z'
  notBefore: '2022-07-13T06:51:51Z'
  renewalTime: '2022-09-11T06:51:51Z'
  revision: 1
```

SealedSecret from bitnami:
```
status:
  conditions:
    - lastTransitionTime: '2022-07-13T06:51:20Z'
      lastUpdateTime: '2022-07-13T06:51:20Z'
      status: 'True'
      type: Synced
```

### Version

18.0.2

### Expected behavior

The Keycloak CRD should report its status using yaml strings:

```yaml
status:
  conditions:
    - message: ''
      status: 'True'
      type: Ready
    - message: ''
      status: 'False'
      type: HasErrors
    - message: ''
      status: 'False'
      type: RollingUpdate
```

### Actual behavior

issue_title: Encoded token stored as KC_RESTART cookie uses weak algorithm- HS256
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

This cookie is an internal cookie that is used by Keycloak. This cookie is used by Keycloak in the authentication flow to begin a new authentication flow if something happens and the flow needs to be restarted (https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak-community/pull/16/files/4c9b955990190344a3f1ed98c0a0a1dd62d1e8ec).

This cookie is classified as an internal cookie in Keycloak code. Here we can see the implementation for this cookie -> https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/main/services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/protocol/RestartLoginCookie.java

On line 184, it can be seen that this cookie's category is set as 'INTERNAL'.

Keycloak has a token manager and this class is used to specify which algorithm should be used for each cookie type (https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/main/services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/jose/jws/DefaultTokenManager.java#L192).

The issue we have is that because the KC_RESTART token has a category of 'INTERNAL', in the token manager class on line 192, it can be seen that it is hardcoded in Keycloak to always use HS256 algorithm for this type of token. HS256 is a weak algorithm.

### Version

17.0.1

### Expected behavior

Either the algorithm should be changed to a secure one or there should be an option to change the algorithm.

### Actual behavior

The token stored as KC_RESTART cookie uses weak algorithm HS256

### How to Reproduce?

Decode the JWT token stored as KC_RESTART cookie using a Burp Suite Extender extension such as JSON Web Tokens to identify that the algorithm in use 'alg' value is 'HS256'

### Anything else?

_No response_

Increase internal algorithm security using HS512 and 128 byte hmac keys
Closes #13080

issue_title: [AD-LDS] - Exception when user has password expired. Impossible to update password
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

Hi,

We are using an AD LDS server to manage our users and after upgrading from Keycloak v9.0.3 to 15.1.1 we found an issue with the mapper **MSADLDSUserAccountControlStorageMapper**.

In fact, when we have users with expired password, keycloak throw an exception : 
**Not possible to write 'required action UPDATE_PASSWORD' when updating user 'customer1'**



### Version

15.1.1

### Expected behavior

When user has expired password (**ldap Code 49 / hex 532**) , keycloak should allow him to update password.

### Actual behavior

When user has expired password (**ldap Code 49 / hex 532**) , keycloak throw an exception when adding required action "UPDATE_PASSWORD"

### How to Reproduce?

- Start keycloak v15.1.1 or newer
- Add an user federation with an AD LDS (Mode : WRITABLE / Import User : False)
- Add a msad-lds-user-account-control-mapper
- Create a user.
- Create an expiration password policy very short (easy to reproduce)
- Login with user after the password's expiration 

User needs to have an expired password and not only a lastPwdSet = 0.


### Anything else?

Issue is with the **MSADLDSUserAccountControlStorageMapper** which do no query **msDS-UserPasswordExpired** attribut in ldap query. So attribut is not send back in query result : 
https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/15.1.1/federation/ldap/src/main/java/org/keycloak/storage/ldap/mappers/msadlds/MSADLDSUserAccountControlStorageMapper.java#L62

When an user has an expired password  **MSADLDSUserAccountControlStorageMapper** looks if required action UPDATE_PASSWORD is present on the user otherwise it try to add it.
https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/15.1.1/federation/ldap/src/main/java/org/keycloak/storage/ldap/mappers/msadlds/MSADLDSUserAccountControlStorageMapper.java#L135

The condition for required action already present is pwdLastSet=0 OR msDS-UserPasswordExpired present : 
https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/15.1.1/federation/ldap/src/main/java/org/keycloak/storage/ldap/mappers/msadlds/MSADLDSUserAccountControlStorageMapper.java#L266

The condition is false because **msDS-UserPasswordExpired** is not present and pwdLastSet > 0.

**MSADLDSUserAccountControlStorageMapper** try to add UPDATE_PASSWORD action on the user : 
https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/15.1.1/federation/ldap/src/main/java/org/keycloak/storage/ldap/mappers/msadlds/MSADLDSUserAccountControlStorageMapper.java#L136

But the user is an read only model in keycloak v15.1.1 (not the case in v9.0.3) and an exception is raise : 
https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/15.1.1/federation/ldap/src/main/java/org/keycloak/storage/ldap/LDAPStorageProvider.java#L207
https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/6bfd4549bb1e9a17791849252e1de0d95a9f4012/server-spi-private/src/main/java/org/keycloak/models/utils/ReadOnlyUserModelDelegate.java#L71


**Solution :** 
Submited a PR : https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/pull/13086

issue_title: JPA Map storage concurrent execution of liquibase updates
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

There are currently two synchronization problems: 

The following code will add the current model to the list of `validatedModels`. A second request would not wait here while the Liquibase upgrade is performed, and would instead overtake the first request and would fail due to missing tables. 

https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/f6a2b334d1e0652dcaba76cf8fccdc464a3171cd/model/map-jpa/src/main/java/org/keycloak/models/map/storage/jpa/JpaMapStorageProviderFactory.java#L377-L398

A second problem arises as the databasechangelog table is not locked with Quarkus. With undertow, as long as the legacy store is in the classpath, the [CustomLockService](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/7c1d6eae431be5bcea976e67e099c8fe4391118d/model/jpa/src/main/java/org/keycloak/connections/jpa/updater/liquibase/lock/CustomLockService.java) will take care of this. 

To untangle the old JPA legacy store from the new JPA map store, this needs to be duplicated / adjusted to the needs to JPA map storage.


### Version

999-SNAPSHOT

### Expected behavior

When one thread migrates the database, all other threads should wait for that to complete. 

### Actual behavior

Threads might overtake each other, and even try to do parallel deployments.

### How to Reproduce?

Debugging with the Quarkus distribution.

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Fall back to standard Liquibase locking

As DBLockProvider is "none" for the Map storage providers, there is no locking provided by DB Lock
provider.

Liquibase's classic lock provider has issues that need to be tackled in a follow-up issue, see https://github.com/liquibase/liquibase/issues/1311

Closes #13130

Introduced by this commit: https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/commit/90947404a5f5a3b884aca95492c09f65adf9af14

### Version

15.1.0

### Expected behavior

If a user with an existing Keycloak account uses Google as an OIDC Identity Provider for the first time, where the Google Identity's email is the same as Keycloak's, a prompt stating the "Account already exists" will be presented to the user.  If the user clicks `Add to existing account` an `Identity Provider Link` is created and the user is able to access the protected resource (if authorized).

### Actual behavior

If a user with an existing Keycloak account authenticates using Google as an OIDC Identity Provider for the first time, where the Google Identity's email is the same as Keycloak's, a prompt stating the "Account already exists" will be presented to the user.  If the user clicks `Add to existing account` this error is thrown on the backend and the UI tells the user "We are sorry... Unexpected error when handling authentication request to identity provider." The `Identity Provider Link` is not being created and the user is thus unable to use the OIDC IDP for AuthN.

### How to Reproduce?

_No response_

### Anything else?

[This line](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/442eff01691c5dba44ee334c0ae3652921080187/services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/email/freemarker/FreeMarkerEmailTemplateProvider.java#L152) should also check if `idpDisplayName` string length is greater than 0.

```java
if (idpDisplayName  != null && idpDisplayName .length() > 0) {
    idpAlias = ObjectUtil.capitalize(idpDisplayName);
}
```

issue_title: Default required action providers are still available after feature disabling
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

In the testsuite, when the `@DisableFeature` annotation is used and the provider for a particular required action is used as the default one, it's not possible to continue with an authentication process. It's caused by the fact, that the provider still exists in the realm, but it's not possible to find a related provider factory for it due to the factory is not initialized, when the feature is disabled (implements `EnvironmentDependentProviderFactory`). 

When the annotation is used, the server shouldn't throw 500, when the particular factory is not found.

issue_body:
### Describe the bug

**org.keycloak.testsuite.model.ConcurrentTransactionsTest** presents failures/errors with `CockroachDB`:

```
[ERROR]   ConcurrentTransactionsTest.persistClient » RunOnServer java.lang.NullPointerException
```

- Required JPA stores to reproduce the failures: **realms**

The specific point where this happens follows:

 ```
 Caused by: java.lang.NullPointerException
	at org.keycloak.models.map.user.MapUserProvider.getUserByUsername(MapUserProvider.java:490)
	at org.keycloak.testsuite.model.ConcurrentTransactionsTest.tearDownRealm(ConcurrentTransactionsTest.java:284)
	at org.keycloak.testsuite.model.ConcurrentTransactionsTest.persistClient(ConcurrentTransactionsTest.java:192)

In this specific case, the `realm` that is being supplied to `getUserByUsername` is `null` - the test calls `session.realms().getRealmByName("original")` and this is returning `null`, even though the entry **exists** in the database (which can be verified by setting a breakpoint in `MapRealmProvider.getRealmByName()` and querying the DB right before hibernate does so.

main

_No response_

1- Setup CockroachDB according to the instructions in https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/discussions/9596

```
cd testsuite/integration-arquillian

mvn clean install -Pmap-storage -Dpageload.timeout=90000 -Dkeycloak.realm.map.storage.provider=jpa \
-Dkeycloak.map.storage.connectionsJpa.url=jdbc:postgresql://localhost:26257/keycloak \

issue_title: Followup: JPA Map store wants to use `hibernate.integrator_provider` in Quarkus
issue_body:
### Description

When implementing the JPA Map store in Quarkus, in issue #13200 it was found that the Hibernate Event Listeners didn't register. 

This seems to be due to the property `hibernate.integrator_provider` not being supported in Quarkus, while it worked fine in Undertow. 

While the issue implemented a working solution, it wasn't as isolated as we wished for. This is a follow-up issue to report this upstream and to look for a better solution.

### Discussion

_No response_

### Motivation

Avoid a global serviceloader, when the event listener should be set up in isolated way for just for the JPA Map storage entitymanagers. 

Having it registered for all entity managers, it might negatively affect other storage implementation which are not JPA Map storage but still use JPA.

### Details

The current service loader class is `EventListenerIntegrator`.
commit_message:
Make the event listeners specific to the persistence unit

Closes #13219

issue_title: Followup: Revisit JTA vs. RESOURCE_LOCAL for JPA map storage for Quarkus and other Setups
issue_body:
### Description

When implementing the JPA Map store in Quarkus, in issue #13200 it was found that the autocommit for the DB connection wasn't disabled.

In the Undertow setup for JPA Map storage, Hibernate disabled the autocommit. In the Quarkus setup with the legacy store this is disabled by the XA transaction manger. 

A workaround in the `QuarkusJpaMapStorageProviderFactory` disables the autocommit. 

A better solution would be to disable it in the Agroal connection pool, as that will reset the autocommit on every returned connection, which seems to be overhead. 

Unfortunately I didn't find an option to change the behavior of any component involved, so there is a workaround in place.


### Discussion

_No response_

### Motivation

Avoid switching the autocommit manually back and forth when retrieving and returning the connection. Ideally it should be handled in the connection pool. 

This is also a solution advocated for here: https://vladmihalcea.com/why-you-should-always-use-hibernate-connection-provider_disables_autocommit-for-resource-local-jpa-transactions/

### Details

_No response_
commit_message:
Changing Quarkus transaction handling for JPA map storage to JTA

This has been recommended as the supported way of transaction handling by the Quarkus team.
Adding handling of exceptions thrown when committing JTA.
Re-adding handling of exceptions when interacting with the entity manager, plus wrapping access to queries to map exceptions during auto-flushing.

Closes #13222

issue_title: Username is removed when updating service account with empty/null email when declarative user profile and registrationEmailAsUsername is enabled
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

What appears to be happening here is that when a UserProfile is auto-constructed for a User on lookup, there is a bug in a check which results in the username being assigned null if email is not set (causing an email attribute to exist but be an empty list).

https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/18.0.2/server-spi-private/src/main/java/org/keycloak/userprofile/DefaultAttributes.java#L308

```java
// XXX: email may be non-null but empty list
List<String> email = newAttributes.get(UserModel.EMAIL);

if (email != null && realm.isRegistrationEmailAsUsername()) {
    final List<String> lowerCaseEmailList = email.stream()
            .filter(Objects::nonNull)
            .map(String::toLowerCase)
            .collect(Collectors.toList());
    // XXX: username overwritten with empty list
    newAttributes.put(UserModel.USERNAME, lowerCaseEmailList);
}
```

Then, subsequently when update to the User entity is made, a difference is detected between the current (set) username value and the incoming (now unset because derived from empty email), and the username is annulled

![1-emptyusernameattr](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/77031/180072382-88129672-0e43-413f-8994-bd2bb9aafab8.png)

![2-setnullusername](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/77031/180072385-0266bfea-7219-4964-a809-93f64c69c289.png)
.

(screenshots are depicting different users because of separate debug runs, but this sequence can happen to each user)

### Version

18.0.0

### Expected behavior

`username` field is not set to null/empty when email is not set for user account, or `registrationEmailAsUsername` is not applied to service account users.

### Actual behavior

`username` field on user is unexpected set from valid value to null.

### How to Reproduce?

use `PUT PUT /admin/realms/{realm}/users/{id}` API to update a service-account user which has a username, but no email, when realm has `"registrationEmailAsUsername": true`

### Anything else?

Discovered on 18.0.0

issue_title: Performance Issues with many offline sessions
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

We have approx. 6 million offline sessions in the database and enabled lazy loading of offline sessions. 

We ran into performance issues: 

If a user has >= 2 user sessions  and you open the consents tab in the admin UI for this user, the following code tries to load the corresponding offline client sessions: 

https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/febb447919044943241ad97200821c7da5099cb3/model/jpa/src/main/java/org/keycloak/models/jpa/session/JpaUserSessionPersisterProvider.java#L383

The where clause: 

sess.userSessionId >= :fromSessionId and sess.userSessionId <= :toSessionId

can cause the query to load lots of client sesssions. 

In our test the user had 2 user sessions with the IDs 156db9e9-d2ad-4168-89ba-21818a475dcc' and 'd4fed476-52c3-44d6-a510-7db0158dc032'

The query tried to load about 3 million client sessions. 

The long-running query leads to infinispan timeout exceptions and can even cause the kubernetes pods to get unhealthy.

The where clause had been changed from an IN-clause to the above BETWEEN-clause in this PR: https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/pull/8012/files#diff-9e427660baeacfeb4c94e0dcbede5ae96e7be4355554023fcd204118e75fe40dL269





### Version

15.1.1

### Expected behavior

Offline user sessions are displayed in the user's consent tab and can be revoked.

### Actual behavior

Click on user consents tab causes severe performacance issues and infinispan timeout exceptions and may even cause pods to get unhealthy or the service to crash completely.

### How to Reproduce?

- a lot of offline sessions (> 1 million)
- enable lazy loading of offline sessions
- create 2 offline sessions for 1 user (login with scope offline_access)
- open admin UI, open user, click on consents tab

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Fix performance issues with many offline sessions

Fixes: #13340

issue_title: Jpa Map Storage: locked user sessions lead to read timeouts
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

When running the broker tests, the logout process ends up with exceptions like the one bellow:

```
23:15:40,417 WARN  [org.keycloak.broker.oidc.OIDCIdentityProvider] Failed backchannel broker logout to: https://localhost:8543/auth/realms/provider/protocol/openid-connect/logout?state=2af21a4b-5222-452e-9339-cd76999045a8&id_token_hint=eyJhbGciOiJSUzI1NiIsInR5cCIgOiAiSldUIiwia2lkIiA6ICJhWWxYbVFhbERjVWM1d2JFNVBON0RKSURGRTQzXzNvZEpJQ2cyNFlFaWxRIn0..XUR4DCJ_wRWOFz_SU3kfFNyUEx7h8aqr_qn7SJ2oshnSTzGqPJFqLBJMrV-nwT2OefcZPly8NphmkvC_xzoJyqSbaY8gY-UbUaGZyd4OZPGSySL8V-aEQHQoKhSC5L6xn0_e-FgFDMgmrmtjlc_tVen4n4-pYxnf9V8LuP973IrZ5zTrRd4RMV9FxLH2WaOu3CrYv581LlSxEdz6bAlkKKz9L500QkokHdUT7qdoWTCykkVujtYlz614-VmUmeEvCEvzB8r_pcUzyQPADEP00r7SnpldzzrEQHVc5Oy0cqzb_RRcAYGK_j61iRB2oV1w0EuaiXUdH2kcagy8P3nDfA
java.net.SocketTimeoutException: Read timed out
	at java.base/java.net.SocketInputStream.socketRead0(Native Method)
	at java.base/java.net.SocketInputStream.socketRead(SocketInputStream.java:115)
	at java.base/java.net.SocketInputStream.read(SocketInputStream.java:168)
	at java.base/java.net.SocketInputStream.read(SocketInputStream.java:140)
```

And this happens because the requests exchanged between the `consumer` and `producer` logout endpoints attempt to lock the same user session:

1. `AbstractBaseBrokerTest.logoutRealm` ends up calling the `LogoutEndpoint.logoutConfirmAction` on the `consumer` endpoint, which proceeds with the browser logout that locks the user session and that at some point sends a logout request to the `provider` endpoint. Relevant call stack up to this point:
```
	at org.keycloak.broker.oidc.OIDCIdentityProvider.backchannelLogout(OIDCIdentityProvider.java:139)
	at org.keycloak.broker.oidc.OIDCIdentityProvider.keycloakInitiatedBrowserLogout(OIDCIdentityProvider.java:155)
	at org.keycloak.services.managers.AuthenticationManager.browserLogout(AuthenticationManager.java:649)
	at org.keycloak.protocol.oidc.endpoints.LogoutEndpoint.initiateBrowserLogout(LogoutEndpoint.java:750)
	at org.keycloak.protocol.oidc.endpoints.LogoutEndpoint.doBrowserLogout(LogoutEndpoint.java:447)
	at org.keycloak.protocol.oidc.endpoints.LogoutEndpoint.logoutConfirmAction(LogoutEndpoint.java:376)
```

2. The `provider` logout endpoint proceeds with the logout until it sends a `k_logout` request back to the `consumer`. Relevant stack:
```
	at org.keycloak.services.managers.ResourceAdminManager.sendLogoutRequest(ResourceAdminManager.java:301)
	at org.keycloak.services.managers.ResourceAdminManager.logoutClientSessions(ResourceAdminManager.java:167)
	at org.keycloak.services.managers.ResourceAdminManager.logoutClientSession(ResourceAdminManager.java:123)
	at org.keycloak.protocol.oidc.OIDCLoginProtocol.backchannelLogout(OIDCLoginProtocol.java:342)
	at org.keycloak.services.managers.AuthenticationManager.backchannelLogoutClientSession(AuthenticationManager.java:509)
	at org.keycloak.services.managers.AuthenticationManager.backchannelLogoutAll(AuthenticationManager.java:393)
	at org.keycloak.services.managers.AuthenticationManager.backchannelLogout(AuthenticationManager.java:296)
	at org.keycloak.services.managers.AuthenticationManager.backchannelLogout(AuthenticationManager.java:253)
	at org.keycloak.protocol.oidc.endpoints.LogoutEndpoint.doBrowserLogout(LogoutEndpoint.java:458)
	at org.keycloak.protocol.oidc.endpoints.LogoutEndpoint.logout(LogoutEndpoint.java:307)
```
3. This request is now handled by the `KeycloakOIDCIdentityProvider.backchannelLogout` endpoint, and at this method, in line 105, attempts to lock the user session
 
```
                    UserSessionModel userSession = lockUserSessionsForModification(session, () -> session.sessions().getUserSessionByBrokerSessionId(realm, brokerSessionId));
```

The request is now waiting for the user session that was locked in step 1, and the deadlock is only undone when the `Read timed out` exception is thrown, wihch allows the first request to proceed and release the lock.

This happens with both PostgreSQL and CockroachDB.

### Version

main

### Expected behavior

Broker logout logic shouldn't get stuck on a deadlock where multiple requests are trying to lock the same user sessions

### Actual behavior

_No response_

### How to Reproduce?

1- Setup CockroachDB according to the instructions in https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/discussions/9596
2- Using the IDE, run any broker test (for example, `ExternalKeycloakRoleToRoleMapperTest`) with the following VM options:

```
-Dpageload.timeout=90000
-Dkeycloak.profile.feature.map_storage=enabled
-Dkeycloak.authSession.provider=map
-Dkeycloak.authorization.provider=map
-Dkeycloak.client.provider=map
-Dkeycloak.clientScope.provider=map
-Dkeycloak.eventsStore.provider=map
-Dkeycloak.group.provider=map
-Dkeycloak.loginFailure.provider=map
-Dkeycloak.realm.provider=map
-Dkeycloak.role.provider=map
-Dkeycloak.actionToken.provider=map
-Dkeycloak.singleUseObject.provider=map
-Dkeycloak.user.provider=map
-Dkeycloak.userSession.provider=map
-Dkeycloak.userSession.map.storage.provider=jpa
-Dkeycloak.publicKeyStorage.provider=map
-Dkeycloak.realmCache.enabled=false
-Dkeycloak.userCache.enabled=false
-Dkeycloak.authorizationCache.enabled=false
-Dkeycloak.publicKeyCache.enabled=false
-Dkeycloak.userFederatedStorage.provider=
-Dkeycloak.userSessionPersister.provider=
-Dkeycloak.dblock.provider=none
-Dkeycloak.map.storage.connectionsJpa.url=jdbc:postgresql://localhost:5432/keycloak
-Dkeycloak.map.storage.connectionsJpa.user=keycloak
-Dkeycloak.map.storage.connectionsJpa.password=pass
```

For CRDB adjust the connection port to 26257

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Fix user session deadlock by enlisting broker logout request after main logout transaction commits. (#13889)

- This also fixes broker test failures with CockroachDB

Closes #13348
Closes #13212
Closes #13214

issue_title: Unable to configure URL scheme for admin console
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

I'm trying to setup Keycloak 18.0.2 in a reverse proxy environment where the HTTPS connection is terminated at the proxy. The admin console is also exposed via the proxy. However, after logging in to the admin console, some requests are blocked with a "Mixed content" error from the browser.

The reason for this appears to be that the scheme of the `authUrl` JavaScript variable is set to `http`:

```
var authServerUrl = 'https://MY-URL/auth';
var authUrl = 'http://MY-URL/auth'; // <-- INCORRECT URL SCHEME
...
```

I could not find a way to configure this. Looking through the code, this line seems suspect: https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/8470a30446cf1a8280353fb96f8e2152d5d186ac/quarkus/runtime/src/main/java/org/keycloak/quarkus/runtime/hostname/DefaultHostnameProvider.java#L67

A recent commit has added the possibility to configure the admin hostname (commit b34f4615, added in https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/pull/12192). Would it be possible to add a similar configuration option for the admin scheme?


### Version

18.0.2
commit_message:
Allow setting a URL to configure frontend and admin URLs

Closes #13524

Co-authored-by: Stian Thorgersen <stianst@gmail.com>

I'm currently trying to deny access to users that are not associated with a specific client role. Following the documentation on https://www.keycloak.org/docs/latest/server_admin/index.html#explicitly-deny-allow-access-in-conditional-flows the flow that I'm trying to use is the following (basically a copy of Direct Grant changing just the OTP conditional):
```
Username Validation   [REQUIRED]
Password   [REQUIRED]
Check Role   [CONDITIONAL]
  Condition - User Role(User MUST HAVE ROLE)   [REQUIRED]
  Deny Access   [REQUIRED]
```

Is this a bug or am I missing something?

### Version

18.0.2

### Expected behavior

Getting an unauthorized error when trying to authenticate a user that doesn't have the required role.

### Actual behavior

commit_message:
Error handle for the Json request in createErrorPage

Closes #13368

These changes introduce a new error handler for building error based on the media type.
- It should create error form response when it is valid HTML request
- It could create error response with JSON if content type matches

Signed-off-by: Lex Cao <lexcao@foxmail.com>

issue_body:
### Description

Running keycloak after the build for optimization command is wrong in the link below.
[https://www.keycloak.org/server/configuration](https://www.keycloak.org/server/configuration)

issue_title: Test Pipelines are unable to fetch Keycloak-admin-UI from Sonatype Repo
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

Keycloakk QE test pipelines use keycloak- version with current timestamp to differentiate between different builds but the current upstream repo fetches the keycloak-admin-ui from NIghhtly releases with a version 999-Snapshot which causes the failure.

### Version

19.0.0

### Expected behavior

Keycloak should be buit successfuly in the test pipelines

### Actual behavior

Following errors occurs in the test pipelines:
`[ERROR] Failed to execute goal on project integration-arquillian-servers-auth-server-undertow: Could not resolve dependencies for project org.keycloak.testsuite:integration-arquillian-servers-auth-server-undertow:jar:999-SNAPSHOT-pr-13393-20220728-124402: Could not find artifact org.keycloak:keycloak-admin-ui:jar:999-SNAPSHOT -> [Help 1]`

### How to Reproduce?

run the command :

mvn clean install -Pdistribution

### Anything else?

_No response_
